title: ‘Imagine an automated torture chamber’: I visited Samsung's South Korea factory to see how its Galaxy foldables are tested — and these robots don't miss a beat
excerpt: We traveled to South Korea and saw how Samsung tests its latest foldables, the Galaxy Z Fold 8, Z Fold 8 Ultra, and Z Flip 8.
content_html:
Imagine an automated torture chamber where one calamity after another is inflicted upon you. Worse yet, it’s all robots doing the pushing, pulling, pressing, switching, and lighting. A robot even delivers you to this house of horrors.

Of course, you’re a Samsung Galaxy phone, and you were actually built to take this kind of punishment, or at least expected to survive it. Housed in a basketball court-sized room, this is one of Samsung’s endlessly running test suites for its growing line of Samsung Galaxy S series and Galaxy Z folding phones.

A few weeks ago, Samsung flew me out to South Korea to visit its Gumi factory where, among other things, they build and stress test their newest phones, including the Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z 8 Flip, all of which were introduced this week at Samsung Galaxy Unpacked in the UK.

As a lab engineer guided us through the space, a robot waited impatiently behind him while trying to deliver more phones to its Guantanamo of tech indignities. The engineer seemed unconcerned as the robot edged closer to us, but it always remained a safe distance away, finally delivering a phone to the first chamber when we’d cleared a path.

Of course, before Samsung checks the viability of these handsets, it builds them.

Make 'em and (try to) break 'em

Image 1 of 3

When I visited the factory — one of nine dotted around the globe and where millions of units are assembled — for the first time a few years ago, I noticed a mix of robots (not humanoid, just automated platforms and a wide variety of robot arms) and humans. This time, however, there were just a handful of humans, who were often checking the robots’ work for imperfections.

As we walked the pristine aisles while wearing thin, blue protective covers on our shoes, I caught the eye of one factory worker and gave her a thumbs up. To my surprise, she smiled and nodded back at me before quickly returning to product inspections.

Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more.

Across a series of metal and plexiglass boxes, I saw all manner of robots and mobile device manufacturing activity. There was never a pause or moment of indecision. In one enclosure, I watched as camera boards were robotically placed in a Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra board and then expertly screwed into place.

Image 1 of 3

Virtually every robot in every enclosure has a specific job, like the one that does nothing but remove thin-blue packing material from a phone circuit board. Its tiny fingers delicately grab a pair of thin blue tabs and strip away the filament. We found ourselves cheering on the little bot.

Image 1 of 5

In another spot, a colleague noticed that a copper line wasn’t properly affixed to a phone’s main circuit board. I could see it bouncing freely and wondered if the error would be caught or end up in someone’s wonky Galaxy device. I needn’t have worried. Samsung’s automated systems are fully capable of both the build and error-catching. As we watched, the offending board shifted off course and was held at bay while other properly built boards moved along.

I saw many different enclosures with so many automated manufacturing capabilities that it was mind-boggling.

Box 'em up but test them first

Image 1 of 4

In another space, I saw sensors identifying phone IMEI numbers, while another robot printed out corresponding labels, and then a third affixed them to boxes that matched the packages to each phone.

Most of the robots I saw were small, but at the end of the line was an almost car-sized palletizer that takes the 10-per-box cardboard crates and stacks them on a pallet, placing the boxes first around the perimeter and then filling in the middle. The motorcycle-sized arm was remarkably fast and accurate.

Some of the phones never make it to the pallet. Instead, a select few are destined for a special test room where a large horseshoe of enclosures determines if the models in the current batch are fit for purpose.

There are two types of test systems in Gumi: the boxes that run the same test over and over again in a single device, and the U-shaped automated test bay where one phone is run in serial through the entire array of tests. These tests will often replicate what is done repeatedly in other enclosures.

The number of test systems and kinds of tests Samsung runs on its products is impressively comprehensive, though some of the boxes look downright bizarre.

Weird tests, reliable products

Image 1 of 9

Folding and unfolding the devices thousands of times is to be expected, as is pressure testing, but I also saw extreme heat and cold tests. There were also enclosures that do nothing but check AI vision systems, millimeter wave, waterproofing, and a facial recognition chamber with life-like mannequin heads that I can still see when I close my eyes.

There’s even a room where partners like Google can remotely access and run tests on Samsung phones.

More astonishing is that this is a living, ever-changing facility. Just imagine what happens every time, like now, Samsung unveils new phones and watches; they must retool. Walking the floor, I noticed how the brand-new Galaxy Z Fold 8 was already undergoing rigorous tests. The lab engineer told us, “For every new form factor, we develop new technology for it,” and they develop new automation to test it.

Look, I’m not saying other companies don’t put their mobile hardware through similar wringers, but I’m not sure any other tech giant has been so transparent about their process. It’s wild, bracing, and I must admit, gives me more than a bit of confidence about each new Galaxy device Samsung delivers.

Secrets of Samsung Display - YouTube

Follow TechRadar on Google News and add us as a preferred source to get our expert news, reviews, and opinion in your feeds.

A 38-year industry veteran and award-winning journalist, Lance has covered technology since PCs were the size of suitcases and “on line” meant “waiting.” He’s a former Lifewire Editor-in-Chief, Mashable Editor-in-Chief, and, before that, Editor in Chief of PCMag.com and Senior Vice President of Content for Ziff Davis, Inc. He also wrote a popular, weekly tech column for Medium called The Upgrade.

Lance Ulanoff makes frequent appearances on national, international, and local news programs including Live with Kelly and Mark, the Today Show, Good Morning America, CNBC, CNN, and the BBC.

blocks.html_en: Imagine an automated torture chamber where one calamity after another is inflicted upon you. Worse yet, it’s all robots doing the pushing, pulling, pressing, switching, and lighting. A robot even delivers you to this house of horrors.

sentences.en: Imagine an automated torture chamber where one calamity after another is inflicted upon you.
sentences.zh: 想象一下一个自动酷刑室，一场又一场的灾难降临到你身上。

sentences.en: Worse yet, it’s all robots doing the pushing, pulling, pressing, switching, and lighting.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，所有的推、拉、压、开关和照明工作都是由机器人完成的。

sentences.en: A robot even delivers you to this house of horrors.
sentences.zh: 机器人甚至会把你带到这座恐怖之屋。

blocks.html_en: Of course, you’re a Samsung Galaxy phone, and you were actually built to take this kind of punishment, or at least expected to survive it. Housed in a basketball court-sized room, this is one of Samsung’s endlessly running test suites for its growing line of Samsung Galaxy S series and Galaxy Z folding phones.

sentences.en: Of course, you’re a Samsung Galaxy phone, and you were actually built to take this kind of punishment, or at least expected to survive it.
sentences.zh: 当然，你是一部三星 Galaxy 手机，你实际上是为了承受这种惩罚而生的，或者至少预计能够幸存下来。

sentences.en: Housed in a basketball court-sized room, this is one of Samsung’s endlessly running test suites for its growing line of Samsung Galaxy S series and Galaxy Z folding phones.
sentences.zh: 这是三星不断运行的测试套件之一，位于一个篮球场大小的房间内，用于不断增长的三星 Galaxy S 系列和 Galaxy Z 折叠手机系列。

blocks.html_en: A few weeks ago, Samsung flew me out to South Korea to visit its Gumi factory where, among other things, they build and stress test their newest phones, including the Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z 8 Flip, all of which were introduced this week at Samsung Galaxy Unpacked in the UK.

sentences.en: A few weeks ago, Samsung flew me out to South Korea to visit its Gumi factory where, among other things, they build and stress test their newest phones, including the Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z 8 Flip, all of which were introduced this week at Samsung Galaxy Unpacked in the UK.
sentences.zh: 几周前，三星带我前往韩国参观其龟尾工厂，除其他外，他们在那里制造和压力测试最新的手机，包括 Galaxy Z Fold 8、Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra 和 Galaxy Z 8 Flip，所有这些手机均于本周在英国的 Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 上推出。

sentences.en: The engineer seemed unconcerned as the robot edged closer to us, but it always remained a safe distance away, finally delivering a phone to the first chamber when we’d cleared a path.
sentences.zh: 当机器人逐渐靠近我们时，工程师似乎并不关心，但它始终保持着安全距离，最终在我们清理出一条道路时将手机送到了第一个房间。

sentences.zh: 当然，在三星检查这些手机的可行性之前，它会先生产它们。

sentences.zh: 然而，这一次只有少数人类，他们经常检查机器人的工作是否有缺陷。

blocks.html_en: As we walked the pristine aisles while wearing thin, blue protective covers on our shoes, I caught the eye of one factory worker and gave her a thumbs up. To my surprise, she smiled and nodded back at me before quickly returning to product inspections.

sentences.en: To my surprise, she smiled and nodded back at me before quickly returning to product inspections.
sentences.zh: 令我惊讶的是，她微笑着向我点点头，然后迅速返回产品检查。

sentences.zh: 摩托车大小的手臂非常快速和准确。

sentences.zh: 有些手机从未上货。

sentences.en: Instead, a select few are destined for a special test room where a large horseshoe of enclosures determines if the models in the current batch are fit for purpose.
sentences.zh: 相反，少数被选中的模型将被送往一个特殊的测试室，在那里，一大片马蹄形的外壳决定了当前批次的模型是否适合用途。

sentences.en: There are two types of test systems in Gumi: the boxes that run the same test over and over again in a single device, and the U-shaped automated test bay where one phone is run in serial through the entire array of tests.
sentences.zh: 龟尾有两种类型的测试系统：在单个设备中反复运行相同测试的盒子，以及 U 形自动测试台，其中一部手机串行运行整个测试阵列。

sentences.en: These tests will often replicate what is done repeatedly in other enclosures.
sentences.zh: 这些测试通常会重复在其他机柜中重复执行的操作。

blocks.html_en: The number of test systems and kinds of tests Samsung runs on its products is impressively comprehensive, though some of the boxes look downright bizarre.

sentences.zh: 三星在其产品上运行的测试系统数量和测试种类令人印象深刻，尽管有些盒子看起来非常奇怪。

sentences.zh: 第 1 张图片（共 9 张）

sentences.en: Getting a close look at one of the enclosures (Image credit: Samsung)
sentences.zh: 仔细观察其中一个外壳（图片来源：三星）

blocks.html_en: Folding and unfolding the devices thousands of times is to be expected, as is pressure testing, but I also saw extreme heat and cold tests. There were also enclosures that do nothing but check AI vision systems, millimeter wave, waterproofing, and a facial recognition chamber with life-like mannequin heads that I can still see when I close my eyes.

sentences.en: Folding and unfolding the devices thousands of times is to be expected, as is pressure testing, but I also saw extreme heat and cold tests.
sentences.zh: 折叠和展开设备数千次是可以预料的，压力测试也是如此，但我也看到了极热和极冷的测试。

sentences.en: There were also enclosures that do nothing but check AI vision systems, millimeter wave, waterproofing, and a facial recognition chamber with life-like mannequin heads that I can still see when I close my eyes.
sentences.zh: 还有一些外壳，除了检查人工智能视觉系统、毫米波、防水和带有栩栩如生的人体模型头部的面部识别室外，什么也不做，当我闭上眼睛时，我仍然可以看到它们。

blocks.html_en: There’s even a room where partners like Google can remotely access and run tests on Samsung phones.

sentences.en: There’s even a room where partners like Google can remotely access and run tests on Samsung phones.
sentences.zh: 甚至还有一个房间，谷歌等合作伙伴可以在三星手机上远程访问和运行测试。

blocks.html_en: More astonishing is that this is a living, ever-changing facility. Just imagine what happens every time, like now, Samsung unveils new phones and watches; they must retool. Walking the floor, I noticed how the brand-new Galaxy Z Fold 8 was already undergoing rigorous tests. The lab engineer told us, “For every new form factor, we develop new technology for it,” and they develop new automation to test it.

sentences.en: More astonishing is that this is a living, ever-changing facility.
sentences.zh: 更令人惊讶的是，这是一个活生生的、不断变化的设施。

sentences.en: Just imagine what happens every time, like now, Samsung unveils new phones and watches; they must retool.
sentences.zh: 想象一下每次三星推出新手机和手表时会发生什么，就像现在一样；他们必须重新装备。

sentences.en: Walking the floor, I noticed how the brand-new Galaxy Z Fold 8 was already undergoing rigorous tests.
sentences.zh: 走在地板上，我注意到全新的 Galaxy Z Fold 8 已经在接受严格的测试。

sentences.en: The lab engineer told us, “For every new form factor, we develop new technology for it,” and they develop new automation to test it.
sentences.zh: 实验室工程师告诉我们，“对于每一种新的外形尺寸，我们都会为其开发新技术”，他们开发新的自动化来测试它。

blocks.html_en: Look, I’m not saying other companies don’t put their mobile hardware through similar wringers, but I’m not sure any other tech giant has been so transparent about their process. It’s wild, bracing, and I must admit, gives me more than a bit of confidence about each new Galaxy device Samsung delivers.

sentences.en: Look, I’m not saying other companies don’t put their mobile hardware through similar wringers, but I’m not sure any other tech giant has been so transparent about their process.
sentences.zh: 听着，我并不是说其他​​公司不会让他们的移动硬件经历类似的考验，但我不确定是否有其他科技巨头对其流程如此透明。

sentences.en: It’s wild, bracing, and I must admit, gives me more than a bit of confidence about each new Galaxy device Samsung delivers.
sentences.zh: 它是狂野的、令人振奋的，我必须承认，这让我对三星推出的每一款新 Galaxy 设备都充满了信心。

blocks.html_en: A 38-year industry veteran and award-winning journalist, Lance has covered technology since PCs were the size of suitcases and “on line” meant “waiting.” He’s a former Lifewire Editor-in-Chief, Mashable Editor-in-Chief, and, before that, Editor in Chief of PCMag.com and Senior Vice President of Content for Ziff Davis, Inc. He also wrote a popular, weekly tech column for Medium called The Upgrade.

sentences.en: A 38-year industry veteran and award-winning journalist, Lance has covered technology since PCs were the size of suitcases and “on line” meant “waiting.”
sentences.zh: 兰斯是一位拥有 38 年行业经验的资深人士和屡获殊荣的记者，自从个人电脑只有手提箱大小而“在线”意味着“等待”以来，他一直在报道技术。

sentences.en: He’s a former Lifewire Editor-in-Chief, Mashable Editor-in-Chief, and, before that, Editor in Chief of PCMag.com and Senior Vice President of Content for Ziff Davis, Inc. He also wrote a popular, weekly tech column for Medium called The Upgrade.
sentences.zh: 他曾担任 Lifewire 的主编、Mashable 的主编，此前还担任过 PCMag.com 的主编和 Ziff Davis, Inc. 的内容高级副总裁。他还为 Medium 撰写了一篇颇受欢迎的每周科技专栏，名为“The Upgrade”。

blocks.html_en: Lance Ulanoff makes frequent appearances on national, international, and local news programs including Live with Kelly and Mark, the Today Show, Good Morning America, CNBC, CNN, and the BBC.

sentences.en: Lance Ulanoff makes frequent appearances on national, international, and local news programs including Live with Kelly and Mark, the Today Show, Good Morning America, CNBC, CNN, and the BBC.
sentences.zh: 兰斯·乌兰诺夫 (Lance Ulanoff) 经常出现在国内、国际和地方新闻节目中，包括《Live with Kelly and Mark》、《Today Show》、《早安美国》、CNBC、CNN 和 BBC。

content_html_zh:
想象一下一个自动酷刑室，一场又一场的灾难降临到你身上。 更糟糕的是，所有的推、拉、压、开关和照明工作都是由机器人完成的。 机器人甚至会把你带到这座恐怖之屋。

当然，你是一部三星 Galaxy 手机，你实际上是为了承受这种惩罚而生的，或者至少预计能够幸存下来。 这是三星不断运行的测试套件之一，位于一个篮球场大小的房间内，用于不断增长的三星 Galaxy S 系列和 Galaxy Z 折叠手机系列。

几周前，三星带我前往韩国参观其龟尾工厂，除其他外，他们在那里制造和压力测试最新的手机，包括 Galaxy Z Fold 8、Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra 和 Galaxy Z 8 Flip，所有这些手机均于本周在英国的 Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 上推出。

当一名实验室工程师引导我们穿过这个空间时，一个机器人在他身后不耐烦地等待着，同时试图向关塔那摩的科技侮辱运送更多的手机。 当机器人逐渐靠近我们时，工程师似乎并不关心，但它始终保持着安全距离，最终在我们清理出一条道路时将手机送到了第一个房间。

当然，在三星检查这些手机的可行性之前，它会先生产它们。

创造它们并（尝试）打破它们

第 1 张图片（共 3 张）

几年前，当我第一次参观这家工厂时（分布在全球的九家工厂之一，组装了数百万个单元），我第一次注意到机器人（不是人形机器人，只是自动化平台和各种机器人手臂）和人类的混合体。 然而，这一次只有少数人类，他们经常检查机器人的工作是否有缺陷。

当我们穿着薄薄的蓝色防护鞋走在原始的过道上时，我引起了一位工厂工人的注意，并向她竖起了大拇指。 令我惊讶的是，她微笑着向我点点头，然后迅速返回产品检查。

订阅突发新闻、评论、观点、顶级技术交易等。

穿过一系列金属和有机玻璃盒子，我看到了各种各样的机器人和移动设备制造活动。 从来没有停顿或犹豫不决的时刻。 在一个外壳中，我看到相机板被自动放置在 Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra 板上，然后熟练地用螺丝固定到位。

第 1 张图片（共 3 张）

事实上，每个外壳中的每个机器人都有特定的工作，比如除了从电话电路板上去除蓝色薄包装材料之外什么都不做的机器人。 它的小手指小心地抓住一对薄薄的蓝色标签并剥掉灯丝。 我们发现自己为这个小机器人欢呼。

第 1 张图片（共 5 张）

在另一个地方，一位同事注意到铜线没有正确固定到手机的主电路板上。 我可以看到它自由地弹跳，想知道错误是否会被捕获或最终出现在某人不稳定的 Galaxy 设备中。 我不必担心。 三星的自动化系统完全有能力进行构建和错误捕获。 正如我们所看到的，有问题的木板偏离了航线并被挡住了，而其他正确建造的木板则继续移动。

我看到许多不同的外壳具有如此多的自动化制造能力，令人难以置信。

把它们装箱，但先测试一下

第 1 张图片（共 4 张）

在另一个空间，我看到传感器识别手机 IMEI 号码，而另一个机器人则打印出相应的标签，然后第三个机器人将它们贴在与每部手机的包裹相匹配的盒子上。

我看到的大多数机器人都很小，但在生产线的末端有一个几乎与汽车大小的码垛机，它接收每箱 10 个的纸板箱并将它们堆放在托盘上，首先将箱子放在周边，然后在中间填充。 摩托车大小的手臂非常快速和准确。

有些手机从未上货。 相反，少数被选中的模型将被送往一个特殊的测试室，在那里，一大片马蹄形的外壳决定了当前批次的模型是否适合用途。

龟尾有两种类型的测试系统：在单个设备中反复运行相同测试的盒子，以及 U 形自动测试台，其中一部手机串行运行整个测试阵列。 这些测试通常会重复在其他机柜中重复执行的操作。

三星在其产品上运行的测试系统数量和测试种类令人印象深刻，尽管有些盒子看起来非常奇怪。

奇怪的测试，可靠的产品

第 1 张图片（共 9 张）

折叠和展开设备数千次是可以预料的，压力测试也是如此，但我也看到了极热和极冷的测试。 还有一些外壳，除了检查人工智能视觉系统、毫米波、防水和带有栩栩如生的人体模型头部的面部识别室外，什么也不做，当我闭上眼睛时，我仍然可以看到它们。

甚至还有一个房间，谷歌等合作伙伴可以在三星手机上远程访问和运行测试。

更令人惊讶的是，这是一个活生生的、不断变化的设施。 想象一下每次三星推出新手机和手表时会发生什么，就像现在一样；他们必须重新装备。 走在地板上，我注意到全新的 Galaxy Z Fold 8 已经在接受严格的测试。 实验室工程师告诉我们，“对于每一种新的外形尺寸，我们都会为其开发新技术”，他们开发新的自动化来测试它。

听着，我并不是说其他​​公司不会让他们的移动硬件经历类似的考验，但我不确定是否有其他科技巨头对其流程如此透明。 它是狂野的、令人振奋的，我必须承认，这让我对三星推出的每一款新 Galaxy 设备都充满了信心。

三星显示器的秘密 - YouTube

在 Google 新闻上关注 TechRadar，并将我们添加为首选来源，以便在您的 Feed 中获取我们的专家新闻、评论和意见。

兰斯是一位拥有 38 年行业经验的资深人士和屡获殊荣的记者，自从个人电脑只有手提箱大小而“在线”意味着“等待”以来，他一直在报道技术。 他曾担任 Lifewire 的主编、Mashable 的主编，此前还担任过 PCMag.com 的主编和 Ziff Davis, Inc. 的内容高级副总裁。他还为 Medium 撰写了一篇颇受欢迎的每周科技专栏，名为“The Upgrade”。

兰斯·乌兰诺夫 (Lance Ulanoff) 经常出现在国内、国际和地方新闻节目中，包括《Live with Kelly and Mark》、《Today Show》、《早安美国》、CNBC、CNN 和 BBC。
